assertions.title: Sort by assessed value works correctly
assertions.reason: On the dedicated /fixtures/sort-nulls surface, selecting 'Assessed Value (high→low)' reordered the table so all non-null assessed values appeared first in descending order (Leo 1,200,000; Alice 650,000; David 510,000; Karen 475,000; Bob 420,000), and null-assessed rows began only after those non-null rows (Carol then Elena, followed by other null-assessed leads).

steps.action: Open http://localhost:5173/fixtures/sort-nulls and confirm the instructional fixture banner for VAL-DASH-008 is present.
steps.expected: Fixture page loads with mixed null/non-null assessed values and the Assessed Value sort option available.
steps.observed: The fixture banner described VAL-DASH-008 and the Sort dropdown included 'Assessed Value (high→low)'.

steps.action: Select 'Assessed Value (high→low)' from the Sort dropdown.
steps.expected: Rows reorder by assessed value descending with null assessed values sorted to the bottom.
steps.observed: The selected sort changed to 'Assessed Value (high→low)' and the observed owner order became Leo Chen, Alice Alvarez, David Kowalski, Karen Wright, Bob Nguyen, Carol Ferreira, Elena Rossi, Frank Torres, Grace Kim, Henry Patel, Isabel Santos, Jack Liu.

steps.action: Compare the observed table order against the fixture's mixed null/non-null assessed values.
steps.expected: All non-null assessed values appear before any null-assessed rows, in descending numeric order.
steps.observed: The non-null assessed rows were first and descending; the first null-assessed rows (Carol and Elena) appeared only after Leo/Alice/David/Karen/Bob, satisfying the null-bottom contract.

reproductionNotes: Use the Sort dropdown and select 'Assessed Value (high→low)'.
Verify the top rows are Leo, Alice, David, Karen, and Bob in descending assessed-value order.
Verify the first rows with null assessedValue (Carol and Elena) appear only after all non-null assessed-value rows.

evidence.network: agent-browser network requests reported 'No requests captured'; no blocking network failures were observed on this static local fixture route.

assertions.title: Sort by permit value works correctly
assertions.reason: On the dedicated /fixtures/sort-nulls surface, selecting 'Permit Value (high→low)' reordered the table so all non-null permit values appeared first in descending order (Leo 210,000; Isabel 125,000; Frank 85,000; Karen 67,000; Grace 32,000), and null-permit rows appeared only afterward, with Henry and Jack at the bottom as the fixture contract calls out.

steps.action: Keep the same fixture route open and switch the Sort dropdown to 'Permit Value (high→low)'.
steps.expected: Rows reorder by permit value descending with null permit values at the bottom.
steps.observed: The selected sort changed to 'Permit Value (high→low)' and the observed owner order became Leo Chen, Isabel Santos, Frank Torres, Karen Wright, Grace Kim, Alice Alvarez, Bob Nguyen, Carol Ferreira, David Kowalski, Elena Rossi, Henry Patel, Jack Liu.

steps.action: Compare the observed table order against the fixture's mixed null/non-null permit values.
steps.expected: All non-null permit values appear before any null-permit rows, in descending numeric order.
steps.observed: The non-null permit rows were first and descending; the first rows called out as null-permit in the fixture contract (Henry and Jack) remained at the bottom, after all non-null permit-value rows.

steps.action: Check for console issues after the permit-value flow.
steps.expected: No console errors relevant to the flow.

reproductionNotes: Use the Sort dropdown and select 'Permit Value (high→low)'.
Verify the top rows are Leo, Isabel, Frank, Karen, and Grace in descending permit-value order.
Verify the null-permit rows called out by the fixture (Henry and Jack) appear at the bottom after all non-null permit-value rows.

evidence.network: agent-browser network requests reported 'No requests captured'; no blocking network failures were observed on this static local fixture route.

summary: Tested VAL-DASH-008 and VAL-DASH-010 on /fixtures/sort-nulls in browser session f1001c1f54b5__u1. Both assertions passed: assessed-value and permit-value descending sorts kept all non-null values ahead of null rows, with Henry/Jack remaining at the bottom for permit-value and Carol/Elena first appearing only after all non-null assessed-value rows.
